Located near Tucson International Airport, we have a wide variety of programs and services, including our residential program, medical detox, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/tactical-recovery/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">veterans program</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/family-program/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">family program</a>, and <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/outpatient-services/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">outpatient services</a>, making us a destination for those seeking mental health and addiction treatment across the country. Our treatments are based on a comprehensive assessment ranging in a wide variety of factors to help personalize the treatment plan to each patient’s individual needs.</p>
<p>The fact that you are here may mean that you or a <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/talking-to-loved-one-about-treatment/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">loved one</a> is suffering. If this is the case, please know you have discovered a place of understanding, healing, and hope.</p>

More than 20 million Americans struggle with substance use disorders, often requiring professional care such as <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/medical-detox/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">detox</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/holistic-treatment/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">holistic treatments</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/individual-therapy/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">individual therapy</a>, and <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/group-therapy/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">group therapy</a> to achieve lasting recovery.</p>
</div><a class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-link gdlr-core-info-font" href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/addiction-treatment/" target="_self"  >Addiction Treatment</a></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb  gdlr-core-center-align gdlr-core-stunning-text-caption-above-title"  ><h3 class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-title" style="font-size: 40px ;font-weight: 600 ;text-transform: uppercase ;" ><img src="/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/Cottonwood-Tucson-Graphic-85.png"  width="45" style="margin-bottom: -10px; margin-right: 10px;" alt="Cottonwood Tucson mental health and addiction treatment center in AZ" />Mental Health Treatment</h3><div class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-content"  ><p>Mental health disorders, sometimes referred to as mood disorders or affective disorders, refer to a group of diagnoses whose main characteristic is a disturbance of mood. Over 40 million Americans suffer from some kind of mental health disorder – most commonly <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/depression-treatment-center/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">depression</a> and <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/anxiety-treatment-center/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">anxiety</a> – and seek behavioral health treatment such as mood disorders treatment.</p>
</div><a class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-link gdlr-core-info-font" href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mood-disorder-treatment/" target="_self"  >Mental Health Treatment</a></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb  gdlr-core-center-align gdlr-core-stunning-text-caption-above-title"  ><h3 class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-title" style="font-size: 40px ;font-weight: 600 ;text-transform: uppercase ;" ><img src="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/tactical-recovery-logo-green-graphic-400.png"  width="98" style="margin-bottom: -10px; margin-right: 10px;" alt="Cottonwood Tucson mental health and addiction treatment center in AZ" />Veteran & US Military support</h3><div class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-content"  ><p>Cottonwood Tucson’s Tactical Recovery Program offers specialized, trauma-informed treatment for veterans, active military, and first responders. Our Veteran-Ready team provides culturally competent, holistic care for PTSD, addiction, and other behavioral health challenges. We understand the unique experiences of those who serve and provide a supportive environment for healing in Tucson, Arizona.</p>
</div><a class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-link gdlr-core-info-font" href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/veterans/" target="_self"  >Learn More About Our Tactical Recovery Program</a></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper " style="padding: 75px 0px 75px 0px;"  id="gdlr-core-wrapper-4" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background gdlr-core-parallax gdlr-core-js" style="background-image: url(https://cottonwooddetucson.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/Arizona-Purple-Sunset-with-Saguaro-1800.jpg) ;background-size: cover ;background-repeat: no-repeat ;background-position: top right ;"  data-parallax-speed="0"  ></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-content gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-container clearfix gdlr-core-container" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column gdlr-core-column-45 gdlr-core-column-first"  id="gdlr-core-column-18" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content-margin gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb  gdlr-core-left-align gdlr-core-stunning-text-caption-above-title"  ><div class="gdlr-core-stunning-text-item-content" style="font-size: 24px ;"  ><h4><span style="color: #ffffff;">We’re ready to help.</span></h4>

 Self Assessments 

 
 Bill Pay 

 
 Alumni 

 
 Addiction Education 

 
 Blog 

 
 FAQs 

 
 Glossary 

 
 Talking to a Loved One About Treatment 

 

 Contact 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 Cottonwood Treats Every Patient Like They Are The Only Patient 

Recovery Center with 35+ Years of Integrated Expertise Treating Mental Health & Substance Use Disorders 

Our IOP and PHP Programs are NOW OPEN. Contact us at (888) 727-0441 for more information.

 Learn More Call (888) 727-0441 

 

 
 

Cottonwood Tucson Once Again Recognized as One of America’s Best Addiction Treatment Centers by Newsweek 

For the third year in a row, Cottonwood Tucson has been included in Newsweek’s list of America’s Best Addiction Treatment Centers. This prestigious award highlights the nation’s top treatment centers for addiction based on quality of service, reputation, and accreditation relative to in-state competition. Being honored is a recognition of the years of hard work and commitment provided by the team at our Tucson rehabilitation center. Learn More. 

 

 

Behavioral Health & Substance Use Disorder Treatment in Tucson, Arizona 

Welcome to Cottonwood Tucson, an inpatient holistic health center licensed to treat mental health and substance use disorders . Located near Tucson International Airport, we have a wide variety of programs and services, including our residential program, medical detox, veterans program , family program , and outpatient services , making us a destination for those seeking mental health and addiction treatment across the country. Our treatments are based on a comprehensive assessment ranging in a wide variety of factors to help personalize the treatment plan to each patient’s individual needs.

The fact that you are here may mean that you or a loved one is suffering. If this is the case, please know you have discovered a place of understanding, healing, and hope.

 

Residential Program 

At our inpatient rehabilitation center in Tucson, Arizona, we treat a wide range of mental health disorders, as well as drug, alcohol, and various process addictions. We have been working with co-occurring disorders and dual diagnosis for more than 20 years and have a wide variety of experience in the treatment of complex and difficult issues.

 

 

 

Medical Detox 

Our medically managed detoxification consists of consultation with a medical provider, close nursing supervision, the regular monitoring of vital signs, and a medication protocol to manage the physical distress of withdrawal.

 

 

 

Assessment Program 

CAP (Cottonwood Assessment Program) is a 5-day intensive inpatient addiction and/or mental health assessment program providing a comprehensive evaluation that will focus on the specific needs of the individual in our addiction treatment center.

 

 

 Learn More About Our Programs Contact 

 

 

 

 

 Welcome 

Our Holistic Philosophy: Understanding, Healing & Hope 

Our philosophy of care presumes that physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual aspects of life are closely interconnected and equally important to the overall health and wellness of a human being.

At Cottonwood Tucson, we recognize that b ehavioral health and addiction issues are not classifications but represent an individual’s experience of pain and suffering. Those who come to our holistic rehabilitation center are valued, supported, and recognized as unique persons who are capable of change.

 About Us Holistic Treatment 

 

 Addiction Treatment

Addiction treatment helps individuals overcome dependence on substances like alcohol , opioids , and other stimulants through evidence-based therapies and support. More than 20 million Americans struggle with substance use disorders, often requiring professional care such as detox , holistic treatments , individual therapy , and group therapy to achieve lasting recovery.

 Addiction Treatment 

 Mental Health Treatment

Mental health disorders, sometimes referred to as mood disorders or affective disorders, refer to a group of diagnoses whose main characteristic is a disturbance of mood. Over 40 million Americans suffer from some kind of mental health disorder – most commonly depression and anxiety – and seek behavioral health treatment such as mood disorders treatment.

 Mental Health Treatment 

 Veteran & US Military support

Cottonwood Tucson’s Tactical Recovery Program offers specialized, trauma-informed treatment for veterans, active military, and first responders. Our Veteran-Ready team provides culturally competent, holistic care for PTSD, addiction, and other behavioral health challenges. We understand the unique experiences of those who serve and provide a supportive environment for healing in Tucson, Arizona.

 Learn More About Our Tactical Recovery Program 

 We’re ready to help. 

 Contact us now for more information on our center for addiction treatment, mental health treatment, and our daily rates. 

 Call (888) 727-0441 

Our Recovery Program Features 

 

 CO-OCCURRING TREATMENT . We find that many of those who enter treatment present with dual or even multiple diagnoses. We provide assessment and treatment of all co-occurring disorders.

 

 TREATING TRAUMA . Our treatment approaches allow individuals to explore and process issues of trauma in an environment of safety and compassion.

 

 FAMILY PROGRAM . Cottonwood’s Family Program provides a structured, safe space, allowing all family members to find their own healing and recovery.

 Contact 

 Information For Alumni 

We Don't Want to Lose Touch With You! 

Cottonwood is going 100% electronic. 

If you wish to continue receiving information about upcoming Alumni Reunions and other events, please be sure to complete the Alumni Announcement Signup Form .

 Alumni Announcement Signup Form 

 

Alumni Meetings

Stay connected with alumni meetings and resources about ongoing care.

 

 

Upcoming Events

Cottonwood Tucson hosts networking and education events throughout the year.

 

 

 

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today. 

 Let Us Help You Get Started. 

 Contact Verify Your Insurance 

 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 (888) 727-0441 

 

 Our Location 
 

Cottonwood Tucson

4110 West Sweetwater Drive

Tucson, AZ 85745

 View on Google Maps 

 

 Useful Links 
 
 
 Learn About Addiction 

 Photo Tour 

 Self Assessment Quizzes 

 Verify Your Insurance 

 Careers 

 Contact Us 

 

 Stay Connected 
 

 

 

 Subscribe to our Newsletter 

 Download Brochure 

© 2026 Cottonwood Tucson | All Rights Reserved | Privacy Policy

Our professional admission coordinators are caring and dedicated in helping individuals and families in crisis and can be reached day or night and will respond to each call within a maximum of 24 hours.</p>

Cottonwood Tucson behavioral health and addiction treatment" width="400" height="200" title="client admissions" /></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper " style="padding: 50px 0px 50px 0px;" data-skin="Cottonwood-1"  id="gdlr-core-wrapper-4" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-content gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-container clearfix gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-full-no-space" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column gdlr-core-column-60 gdlr-core-column-first"  id="gdlr-core-column-4" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content-margin gdlr-core-js " style="margin: 0px 0px 0px 0px;padding: 0px 50px 0px 50px;"   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item gdlr-core-item-pdb clearfix  gdlr-core-left-align gdlr-core-title-item-caption-top gdlr-core-item-pdlr" style="padding-bottom: 20px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-wrap "  ><h3 class="gdlr-core-title-item-title gdlr-core-skin-title  class-test" style="font-size: 30px ;font-weight: 500 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;"  >Five Steps<span class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-divider gdlr-core-skin-divider" ></span></h3></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p><strong>There are five critical steps in Cottonwood’s admissions process.</strong></p>
<p>Steps:</p>
<ol>
<li>Telephone contact with a Cottonwood admission coordinator begins the process. At this time information about the patient’s needs will be discussed. Medical, therapeutic, and family information will be collected to determine the appropriateness of the patient. This is done to make sure that the patient can truly benefit from <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/treatment-programs/">Cottonwood’s program</a>.</li>
<li>Once it is determined that the individual in question would benefit from treatment at Cottonwood finances are discussed. Whether it is a self-pay situation or there is request to seek insurance benefits a financial counselor will explore all possibilities with the patient and family. Financing is available.</li>
<li>At this point a discussion of family involvement and potential extended care options are discussed. Cottonwood is clear on the front end that treatment is the start of a lifetime of work and that additional treatment is needed whether that is extended residential, intensive-outpatient, meetings, or individual counseling.  </li>
<li>Patients are then given a checklist of “Things to know before you check in” (clothes to bring, personal care items, money, visitor information, telephone usage, contraband list, etc.)</li>
<li>The final step is solidifying an admit date and securing travel arrangements.</li>
</ol>
<div class="call-today">
<p>Call for more admissions information and daily rates: <a href="tel:8887270441">(888) 727-0441</a></p>
<p><em>* If at anytime during the admissions process the potential patient or admissions coordinator decides Cottonwood’s behavioral health and addiction rehab in Arizona is not the right choice, a referral to another source for care can and will be given.</em></p>
</div>

Admissions 

 Welcome to Cottonwood Tucson 

 
 

About Admissions 

At Cottonwood Tucson, we respect your decision to seek treatment for yourself or a loved one. We understand that this process can be both difficult and overwhelming. Our admissions counselors will work to provide you with support, education, and information that will aid you in this process. Admissions counselors are available to talk with you every day of the year, offering you consistency, respect, and confidentiality.

A thorough admissions assessment will allow our counselors to identify your treatment needs and provide you with the best possible direction as you make your decisions.

Our Process 

The admission process begins with the patient or patient’s representative contacting a member of Cottonwood’s admissions team. Our professional admission coordinators are caring and dedicated in helping individuals and families in crisis and can be reached day or night and will respond to each call within a maximum of 24 hours.

 

Five Steps 

 There are five critical steps in Cottonwood’s admissions process. 

Steps:

 

Telephone contact with a Cottonwood admission coordinator begins the process. At this time information about the patient’s needs will be discussed. Medical, therapeutic, and family information will be collected to determine the appropriateness of the patient. This is done to make sure that the patient can truly benefit from Cottonwood’s program .

Once it is determined that the individual in question would benefit from treatment at Cottonwood finances are discussed. Whether it is a self-pay situation or there is request to seek insurance benefits a financial counselor will explore all possibilities with the patient and family. Financing is available.

At this point a discussion of family involvement and potential extended care options are discussed. Cottonwood is clear on the front end that treatment is the start of a lifetime of work and that additional treatment is needed whether that is extended residential, intensive-outpatient, meetings, or individual counseling.  

Patients are then given a checklist of “Things to know before you check in” (clothes to bring, personal care items, money, visitor information, telephone usage, contraband list, etc.)

The final step is solidifying an admit date and securing travel arrangements.

 

Call for more admissions information and daily rates: (888) 727-0441 

 * If at anytime during the admissions process the potential patient or admissions coordinator decides Cottonwood’s behavioral health and addiction rehab in Arizona is not the right choice, a referral to another source for care can and will be given. 

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today. 

 Let Us Help You Get Started. 

 (888) 727-0441 Verify Your Insurance

Intensive Outpatient Program participants typically attend several hours of structured individual and group therapy sessions multiple times a week. This structure allows for a deeper exploration of the underlying issues contributing to addiction or mental health issues.</p>

PHPs are more intensive than IOPs. Participants may receive medical care, therapy, and support for several hours, five to seven days a week, while still being able to return home each evening. Partial Hospitalization Programs address mental health and substance use disorders through a combination of group therapy, individual counseling, and medication management. </p>

Going beyond the level of support offered in traditional support groups, IOP and PHP groups are led by Master’s-level therapists and support staff who have extensive experience in mental health treatment and addiction recovery.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Benefits of Our Arizona Outpatient Treatment Services</h3>
<p>Outpatient services for addiction and mental health treatment provide numerous advantages for individuals seeking recovery, including:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Flexibility:</strong> Patients can continue working, attending school, or managing family responsibilities while receiving treatment.</li>
<li><strong>Cost-Effectiveness:</strong> Outpatient programs are generally more affordable than inpatient treatment, making them accessible to more individuals.</li>
<li><strong>Support Systems:</strong> Patients can maintain their support networks of family and friends, which can be crucial for long-term recovery.</li>
<li><strong>Real-World Application:</strong> Outpatient treatment allows individuals to apply the skills and coping strategies they learn in therapy to their everyday lives, fostering real-world recovery.</li>
</ul>

Various therapeutic approaches are utilized to address the unique needs of each patient. While topics vary and rotate through treatment, patients receive cognitive behavioral therapy that focuses on learning how to manage behavior and emotion.</p>
</div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Individual therapy</h3>
<p><a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/individual-therapy/">Individual therapy</a> offers a safe and confidential environment for patients to explore their thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. This one-on-one approach allows for personalized support and the development of coping strategies tailored to the individual’s experiences.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Group therapy</h3>
<p>In addition to individual therapy, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/group-therapy/">group therapy</a> sessions provide a supportive space for individuals to share their experiences with others facing similar challenges. This collective environment fosters connection, understanding, and accountability, which can be instrumental in the recovery process.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Family therapy</h3>
<p>Beyond personal and peer support, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/family/">family therapy</a> plays a crucial role in the healing journey. By involving loved ones in the treatment process, this approach helps to address family dynamics, improve communication, and create a supportive home environment for the individual in recovery.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Other outpatient treatment approaches include:</h3>
<ul>
<li>Lectures and process groups about the stages of change, family systems, and boundary education</li>
<li>Discussions of 12-step concepts for addiction treatment and codependency recovery</li>
<li>Education about coping methods for <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/depression/">depression</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/anxiety/">anxiety</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/complex-trauma/">trauma</a>, and <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/trauma-ptsd/">PTSD</a></li>
<li>Identifying triggers and managing impulsivity, along with exploring the costs and consequences of self-defeating behaviors</li>
</ul>
<p>Engaging in IOP/PHP gives participants better long-term outcomes by preparing them to manage their own mental health and substance use disorders. In addition to teaching clients life skills like budgeting and community resource identification, they also learn coping mechanisms such as yoga, meditation, and recreation therapy.</p>

gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p>A Virtual Intensive Outpatient program can be a beneficial option for many reasons. These programs are flexible and accessible, offering care from virtually anywhere. Our Cottonwood Virtual IOP is designed for those with mental health, substance use, or co-occurring disorders.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Here are some highlights of our Virtual IOP:</h3>
<ul>
<li>Offered three nights a week: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday from 5:30 – 8:30 pm.</li>
<li>Program participation usually lasts 8 to 12 weeks, depending on insurance coverage.</li>
<li>Features a therapeutic processing group, psychoeducation, and skill building.</li>
<li>Utilizes evidence-based modalities including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior</li>
<li>Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), and Moral Reconation Therapy (MRT).</li>
<li>The therapist will schedule a psychosocial assessment following a patient’s first group attendance.</li>
</ul>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Qualifications for admission to the Cottonwood Tucson Virtual IOP program include:</h3>
<ul>
<li>Being at least 18 years old.</li>
<li>A diagnosis of a mental health disorder or substance use disorder.</li>
<li>Being free from Suicidal or Homicidal Ideation</li>
<li>Having symptoms that interfere with social or vocational/educational functioning.</li>
<li>Being ready to step down from a residential or PHP program or step up from weekly individual or group outpatient treatment.</li>
<li>Having a computer with a camera and a microphone.</li>
</ul>
<p>While a Virtual IOP is a great option, it may not be for everyone. If a patient begins to show mental instability during treatment, they will be assessed and referred for a higher level of care.</p>

Outpatient Services for Addiction Treatment & Mental Health 

 Intensive Outpatient Program & Partial Hospitalization Program 

 
 

What Are Outpatient Services? 

Our outpatient substance abuse treatment and mental health services encompass a variety of treatment programs that allow individuals to receive care while living at home or in a supportive environment. Outpatient rehabilitation services are ideal for those who do not require intensive supervision or medical detoxification .

 

Types of Outpatient Rehabilitation Services 

There are two primary types of outpatient services for drug and alcohol addiction treatment and mental health treatment :

 

Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP)

Partial Hospitalization Programs (PHP)

 

 Call Now 

What Are Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP)? 

Intensive Outpatient Programs are designed to provide comprehensive treatment while allowing individuals to maintain their daily lives. Unlike traditional outpatient rehab, IOPs offer a more intensive level of support, making them ideal for those who need more than periodic counseling. Intensive Outpatient Program participants typically attend several hours of structured individual and group therapy sessions multiple times a week. This structure allows for a deeper exploration of the underlying issues contributing to addiction or mental health issues.

What are Partial Hospitalization Programs (PHP)? 

Partial Hospitalization Programs are designed to provide intensive treatment while bridging the gap between inpatient and outpatient care, ideal for those needing comprehensive care but don’t require 24-hour supervision. PHPs are more intensive than IOPs. Participants may receive medical care, therapy, and support for several hours, five to seven days a week, while still being able to return home each evening. Partial Hospitalization Programs address mental health and substance use disorders through a combination of group therapy, individual counseling, and medication management. 

Our Tucson, Arizona, Outpatient Services 

Cottonwood’s intensive outpatient program and partial hospitalization programs in Tucson, Arizona, are designed to build the skills needed for long-term addiction recovery or mental health treatment when residential treatment has been completed or is not necessary. Going beyond the level of support offered in traditional support groups, IOP and PHP groups are led by Master’s-level therapists and support staff who have extensive experience in mental health treatment and addiction recovery.

Benefits of Our Arizona Outpatient Treatment Services

Outpatient services for addiction and mental health treatment provide numerous advantages for individuals seeking recovery, including:

 

 Flexibility: Patients can continue working, attending school, or managing family responsibilities while receiving treatment.

 Cost-Effectiveness: Outpatient programs are generally more affordable than inpatient treatment, making them accessible to more individuals.

 Support Systems: Patients can maintain their support networks of family and friends, which can be crucial for long-term recovery.

 Real-World Application: Outpatient treatment allows individuals to apply the skills and coping strategies they learn in therapy to their everyday lives, fostering real-world recovery.

 

 Call Now 

 

What to Expect from Our Outpatient Services in Tucson, Arizona 

Therapy is central to our Arizona outpatient services, providing individuals with the tools to navigate their recovery journey. Various therapeutic approaches are utilized to address the unique needs of each patient. While topics vary and rotate through treatment, patients receive cognitive behavioral therapy that focuses on learning how to manage behavior and emotion.

Individual therapy

 Individual therapy offers a safe and confidential environment for patients to explore their thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. This one-on-one approach allows for personalized support and the development of coping strategies tailored to the individual’s experiences.

Group therapy

In addition to individual therapy, group therapy sessions provide a supportive space for individuals to share their experiences with others facing similar challenges. This collective environment fosters connection, understanding, and accountability, which can be instrumental in the recovery process.

Family therapy

Beyond personal and peer support, family therapy plays a crucial role in the healing journey. By involving loved ones in the treatment process, this approach helps to address family dynamics, improve communication, and create a supportive home environment for the individual in recovery.

Other outpatient treatment approaches include:

 

Lectures and process groups about the stages of change, family systems, and boundary education

Discussions of 12-step concepts for addiction treatment and codependency recovery

Education about coping methods for depression , anxiety , trauma , and PTSD 

Identifying triggers and managing impulsivity, along with exploring the costs and consequences of self-defeating behaviors

 

Engaging in IOP/PHP gives participants better long-term outcomes by preparing them to manage their own mental health and substance use disorders. In addition to teaching clients life skills like budgeting and community resource identification, they also learn coping mechanisms such as yoga, meditation, and recreation therapy.

 Call Now 

 

Our Tucson, Arizona, Outpatient Services 

A Virtual Intensive Outpatient program can be a beneficial option for many reasons. These programs are flexible and accessible, offering care from virtually anywhere. Our Cottonwood Virtual IOP is designed for those with mental health, substance use, or co-occurring disorders.

Here are some highlights of our Virtual IOP:

 

Offered three nights a week: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday from 5:30 – 8:30 pm.

Program participation usually lasts 8 to 12 weeks, depending on insurance coverage.

Features a therapeutic processing group, psychoeducation, and skill building.

Utilizes evidence-based modalities including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior

Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), and Moral Reconation Therapy (MRT).

The therapist will schedule a psychosocial assessment following a patient’s first group attendance.

 

Qualifications for admission to the Cottonwood Tucson Virtual IOP program include:

 

Being at least 18 years old.

A diagnosis of a mental health disorder or substance use disorder.

Being free from Suicidal or Homicidal Ideation

Having symptoms that interfere with social or vocational/educational functioning.

Being ready to step down from a residential or PHP program or step up from weekly individual or group outpatient treatment.

Having a computer with a camera and a microphone.

 

While a Virtual IOP is a great option, it may not be for everyone. If a patient begins to show mental instability during treatment, they will be assessed and referred for a higher level of care.

Start Your Outpatient Rehabilitation Journey Today 

Take the next step toward lasting addiction recovery today. Whether you need structured outpatient care or the flexibility of our Virtual IOP, we are here to support your journey. Contact us now to learn more about our programs and find the right path for your recovery.

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today. 

 Let Us Help You Get Started. 

 (888) 727-0441 Verify Your Insurance

data-skin="Cottonwood-1"  id="gdlr-core-wrapper-2" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap" style="background-color: #ffffff ;"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-content gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-container clearfix gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-full-no-space" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column gdlr-core-column-60 gdlr-core-column-first"  id="gdlr-core-column-1" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item gdlr-core-item-pdb clearfix  gdlr-core-left-align gdlr-core-title-item-caption-top gdlr-core-item-pdlr" style="padding-bottom: 20px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-wrap "  ><h2 class="gdlr-core-title-item-title gdlr-core-skin-title  class-test" style="font-size: 33px ;font-weight: 500 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;"  >The Effects of Trauma<span class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-divider gdlr-core-skin-divider" ></span></h2></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p>Exposure to trauma can have lifelong effects. Trauma can increase a person’s risk developing depression, compulsive behaviors, personality disorders, and process addictions. It is also a factor in the development of substance use disorders, since traumatized individuals commonly turn to drugs or alcohol as a coping mechanism.</p>
<p>Cottonwood Tucson’s integrated focus on mental health and substance use disorders includes using somatic experiencing as a modality for processing and understanding trauma. This approach allows clients to self-regulate the nervous system’s responses and reclaim a sense of control over the physical expression of their emotions.</p>
</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper " style="padding: 50px 0px 50px 0px;" data-skin="Cottonwood-1"  id="gdlr-core-wrapper-3" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap" style="background-color: #ebe6e5 ;"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-content gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-container clearfix gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-full-no-space" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column gdlr-core-column-36 gdlr-core-column-first"  id="gdlr-core-column-2" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content-margin gdlr-core-js " style="margin: 0px 0px 0px 0px;padding: 0px 50px 0px 50px;"   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item gdlr-core-item-pdb clearfix  gdlr-core-left-align gdlr-core-title-item-caption-top gdlr-core-item-pdlr" style="padding-bottom: 20px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-wrap "  ><h3 class="gdlr-core-title-item-title gdlr-core-skin-title  class-test" style="font-size: 30px ;font-weight: 500 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;"  >What Is Somatic Experiencing?<span class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-divider gdlr-core-skin-divider" ></span></h3></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p>Somatic experiencing is a form of alternative therapy first introduced in 1997 by Dr. Peter Levine, who noted that animals in the wild do not experience long-term negative effects from being threatened by predators. When the immediate threat has been dealt with, the animal discharges the extra arousal energy using mechanisms such as shaking or breathing deeply.</p>
<p>Dr. Levine developed somatic experiencing to help individuals hone their inherent capacity for self-regulation. Often, people struggling to process trauma are dealing with shame and anxiety that traps their unneeded arousal energy in the body. The trapped energy creates a mood disturbances and feelings of low self-worth that lead to mental, emotional, and spiritual disconnection.</p>
<p>Somatic experiencing helps to reset the nervous system and restore equilibrium. The process is collaborative between the patient and therapist, but focuses on bodily feelings and sensations as opposed to talking at length about painful past events. Individuals are encouraged to expand their internal supply of resources, which might include supportive loved ones, happy memories, pleasant physical sensations, or anything that promotes feelings of safety and security. Creating a network of resources makes it easier to navigate the trauma and work through it.</p>
</div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column gdlr-core-column-24"  id="gdlr-core-column-3" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content-margin gdlr-core-js " style="margin: 0px 0px 0px 0px;padding: 0px 50px 0px 0px;"   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-image-item gdlr-core-item-pdb  gdlr-core-center-align gdlr-core-item-pdlr" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-image-item-wrap gdlr-core-media-image  gdlr-core-image-item-style-rectangle" style="border-width: 0px;"  ><img src="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/red-bridge-500x650-1.jpg" alt="red wooden bridge on nature trail - Cottonwood Tucson behavioral health and addiction treatment" width="500" height="650" title="red bridge" /></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper " style="padding: 50px 0px 50px 0px;" data-skin="Cottonwood-1"  id="gdlr-core-wrapper-4" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-content gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-container clearfix gdlr-core-pbf-wrapper-full-no-space" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column gdlr-core-column-60 gdlr-core-column-first"  id="gdlr-core-column-4" ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content-margin gdlr-core-js " style="margin: 0px 0px 0px 0px;padding: 0px 50px 0px 50px;"   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-background-wrap"  ></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item gdlr-core-item-pdb clearfix  gdlr-core-left-align gdlr-core-title-item-caption-top gdlr-core-item-pdlr" style="padding-bottom: 20px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-wrap "  ><h3 class="gdlr-core-title-item-title gdlr-core-skin-title  class-test" style="font-size: 30px ;font-weight: 500 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;"  >Somatic Experiencing at Cottonwood<span class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-divider gdlr-core-skin-divider" ></span></h3></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p>Cottonwood Tucson’s certified somatic experiencing practitioners have completed a rigorous three-year training program. They offer somatic experiencing in both group and individual sessions, including a special women’s group for trauma survivors.</p>
<p>As part of Cottonwood’s full continuum of care for men and women suffering from primary behavioral health disorders or substance use disorders, somatic experiencing is often combined with other forms of psychotherapy and physical therapies to promote holistic healing. For example, clients with drug or alcohol addiction may use yoga or EMDR to promote increased awareness of the body’s response to emotionally charged situations and how this affects their newfound sobriety. Each client’s treatment plan is personalized to fit his or her own unique needs, setting the stage for a lasting recovery.</p>

Somatic Experiencing 

 Treatment at Cottonwood Tucson 

 
 

The Effects of Trauma 

Exposure to trauma can have lifelong effects. Trauma can increase a person’s risk developing depression, compulsive behaviors, personality disorders, and process addictions. It is also a factor in the development of substance use disorders, since traumatized individuals commonly turn to drugs or alcohol as a coping mechanism.

Cottonwood Tucson’s integrated focus on mental health and substance use disorders includes using somatic experiencing as a modality for processing and understanding trauma. This approach allows clients to self-regulate the nervous system’s responses and reclaim a sense of control over the physical expression of their emotions.

What Is Somatic Experiencing? 

Somatic experiencing is a form of alternative therapy first introduced in 1997 by Dr. Peter Levine, who noted that animals in the wild do not experience long-term negative effects from being threatened by predators. When the immediate threat has been dealt with, the animal discharges the extra arousal energy using mechanisms such as shaking or breathing deeply.

Dr. Levine developed somatic experiencing to help individuals hone their inherent capacity for self-regulation. Often, people struggling to process trauma are dealing with shame and anxiety that traps their unneeded arousal energy in the body. The trapped energy creates a mood disturbances and feelings of low self-worth that lead to mental, emotional, and spiritual disconnection.

Somatic experiencing helps to reset the nervous system and restore equilibrium. The process is collaborative between the patient and therapist, but focuses on bodily feelings and sensations as opposed to talking at length about painful past events. Individuals are encouraged to expand their internal supply of resources, which might include supportive loved ones, happy memories, pleasant physical sensations, or anything that promotes feelings of safety and security. Creating a network of resources makes it easier to navigate the trauma and work through it.

 

Somatic Experiencing at Cottonwood 

Cottonwood Tucson’s certified somatic experiencing practitioners have completed a rigorous three-year training program. They offer somatic experiencing in both group and individual sessions, including a special women’s group for trauma survivors.

As part of Cottonwood’s full continuum of care for men and women suffering from primary behavioral health disorders or substance use disorders, somatic experiencing is often combined with other forms of psychotherapy and physical therapies to promote holistic healing. For example, clients with drug or alcohol addiction may use yoga or EMDR to promote increased awareness of the body’s response to emotionally charged situations and how this affects their newfound sobriety. Each client’s treatment plan is personalized to fit his or her own unique needs, setting the stage for a lasting recovery.

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today.

are designed to provide comprehensive treatment while allowing individuals to maintain their daily lives. Unlike traditional outpatient rehab, IOPs offer a more intensive level of support, making them ideal for those who need more than periodic counseling. Intensive Outpatient Program participants typically attend several hours of structured individual and group therapy sessions multiple times a week. This structure allows for a deeper exploration of the underlying issues contributing to addiction or mental health issues.</p>

PHPs are more intensive than IOPs. Participants may receive medical care, therapy, and support for several hours, five to seven days a week, while still being able to return home each evening. Partial Hospitalization Programs address mental health and substance use disorders through a combination of group therapy, individual counseling, and medication management. </p>

Going beyond the level of support offered in traditional support groups, IOP and PHP groups are led by Master’s-level therapists and support staff who have extensive experience in mental health treatment and addiction recovery.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Benefits of Our Arizona Outpatient Treatment Services</h3>
<p>Outpatient services for addiction and mental health treatment provide numerous advantages for individuals seeking recovery, including:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Flexibility:</strong> Patients can continue working, attending school, or managing family responsibilities while receiving treatment.</li>
<li><strong>Cost-Effectiveness:</strong> Outpatient programs are generally more affordable than inpatient treatment, making them accessible to more individuals.</li>
<li><strong>Support Systems:</strong> Patients can maintain their support networks of family and friends, which can be crucial for long-term recovery.</li>
<li><strong>Real-World Application:</strong> Outpatient treatment allows individuals to apply the skills and coping strategies they learn in therapy to their everyday lives, fostering real-world recovery.</li>
</ul>

Various therapeutic approaches are utilized to address the unique needs of each patient. While topics vary and rotate through treatment, patients receive cognitive behavioral therapy that focuses on learning how to manage behavior and emotion.</p>
</div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Individual therapy</h3>
<p><a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/individual-therapy/">Individual therapy</a> offers a safe and confidential environment for patients to explore their thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. This one-on-one approach allows for personalized support and the development of coping strategies tailored to the individual’s experiences.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Group therapy</h3>
<p>In addition to individual therapy, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/group-therapy/">group therapy</a> sessions provide a supportive space for individuals to share their experiences with others facing similar challenges. This collective environment fosters connection, understanding, and accountability, which can be instrumental in the recovery process.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Family therapy</h3>
<p>Beyond personal and peer support, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/programs/family/">family therapy</a> plays a crucial role in the healing journey. By involving loved ones in the treatment process, this approach helps to address family dynamics, improve communication, and create a supportive home environment for the individual in recovery.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Other outpatient treatment approaches include:</h3>
<ul>
<li>Lectures and process groups about the stages of change, family systems, and boundary education</li>
<li>Discussions of 12-step concepts for addiction treatment and codependency recovery</li>
<li>Education about coping methods for <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/depression/">depression</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/anxiety/">anxiety</a>, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/complex-trauma/">trauma</a>, and <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/trauma-ptsd/">PTSD</a></li>
<li>Identifying triggers and managing impulsivity, along with exploring the costs and consequences of self-defeating behaviors</li>
</ul>
<p>Engaging in IOP/PHP gives participants better long-term outcomes by preparing them to manage their own mental health and substance use disorders. In addition to teaching clients life skills like budgeting and community resource identification, they also learn coping mechanisms such as yoga, meditation, and recreation therapy.</p>

gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="font-weight: 400 ;letter-spacing: 0px ;text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p>A Virtual Intensive Outpatient program can be a beneficial option for many reasons. These programs are flexible and accessible, offering care from virtually anywhere. Our Cottonwood Virtual IOP is designed for those with mental health, substance use, or co-occurring disorders.</p>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Here are some highlights of our Virtual IOP:</h3>
<ul>
<li>Offered three nights a week: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday from 5:30 – 8:30 pm.</li>
<li>Program participation usually lasts 8 to 12 weeks, depending on insurance coverage.</li>
<li>Features a therapeutic processing group, psychoeducation, and skill building.</li>
<li>Utilizes evidence-based modalities including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior</li>
<li>Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), and Moral Reconation Therapy (MRT).</li>
<li>The therapist will schedule a psychosocial assessment following a patient’s first group attendance.</li>
</ul>
<h3 style="font-size: 26px;">Qualifications for admission to the Cottonwood Tucson Virtual IOP program include:</h3>
<ul>
<li>Being at least 18 years old.</li>
<li>A diagnosis of a mental health disorder or substance use disorder.</li>
<li>Being free from Suicidal or Homicidal Ideation</li>
<li>Having symptoms that interfere with social or vocational/educational functioning.</li>
<li>Being ready to step down from a residential or PHP program or step up from weekly individual or group outpatient treatment.</li>
<li>Having a computer with a camera and a microphone.</li>
</ul>
<p>While a Virtual IOP is a great option, it may not be for everyone. If a patient begins to show mental instability during treatment, they will be assessed and referred for a higher level of care.</p>

Outpatient Services for Addiction Treatment & Mental Health 

 Intensive Outpatient Program & Partial Hospitalization Program 

 
 

What Are Outpatient Services? 

Our outpatient substance abuse treatment and mental health services encompass a variety of treatment programs that allow individuals to receive care while living at home or in a supportive environment. Outpatient rehabilitation services are ideal for those who do not require intensive supervision or medical detoxification .

 

Types of Outpatient Rehabilitation Services 

There are two primary types of outpatient services for drug and alcohol addiction treatment and mental health treatment :

 

Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP)

Partial Hospitalization Programs (PHP)

 

 Call Now 

What Are Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP)? 

Intensive Outpatient Programs are designed to provide comprehensive treatment while allowing individuals to maintain their daily lives. Unlike traditional outpatient rehab, IOPs offer a more intensive level of support, making them ideal for those who need more than periodic counseling. Intensive Outpatient Program participants typically attend several hours of structured individual and group therapy sessions multiple times a week. This structure allows for a deeper exploration of the underlying issues contributing to addiction or mental health issues.

What are Partial Hospitalization Programs (PHP)? 

Partial Hospitalization Programs are designed to provide intensive treatment while bridging the gap between inpatient and outpatient care, ideal for those needing comprehensive care but don’t require 24-hour supervision. PHPs are more intensive than IOPs. Participants may receive medical care, therapy, and support for several hours, five to seven days a week, while still being able to return home each evening. Partial Hospitalization Programs address mental health and substance use disorders through a combination of group therapy, individual counseling, and medication management. 

Our Tucson, Arizona, Outpatient Services 

Cottonwood’s intensive outpatient program and partial hospitalization programs in Tucson, Arizona, are designed to build the skills needed for long-term addiction recovery or mental health treatment when residential treatment has been completed or is not necessary. Going beyond the level of support offered in traditional support groups, IOP and PHP groups are led by Master’s-level therapists and support staff who have extensive experience in mental health treatment and addiction recovery.

Benefits of Our Arizona Outpatient Treatment Services

Outpatient services for addiction and mental health treatment provide numerous advantages for individuals seeking recovery, including:

 

 Flexibility: Patients can continue working, attending school, or managing family responsibilities while receiving treatment.

 Cost-Effectiveness: Outpatient programs are generally more affordable than inpatient treatment, making them accessible to more individuals.

 Support Systems: Patients can maintain their support networks of family and friends, which can be crucial for long-term recovery.

 Real-World Application: Outpatient treatment allows individuals to apply the skills and coping strategies they learn in therapy to their everyday lives, fostering real-world recovery.

 

 Call Now 

 

What to Expect from Our Outpatient Services in Tucson, Arizona 

Therapy is central to our Arizona outpatient services, providing individuals with the tools to navigate their recovery journey. Various therapeutic approaches are utilized to address the unique needs of each patient. While topics vary and rotate through treatment, patients receive cognitive behavioral therapy that focuses on learning how to manage behavior and emotion.

Individual therapy

 Individual therapy offers a safe and confidential environment for patients to explore their thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. This one-on-one approach allows for personalized support and the development of coping strategies tailored to the individual’s experiences.

Group therapy

In addition to individual therapy, group therapy sessions provide a supportive space for individuals to share their experiences with others facing similar challenges. This collective environment fosters connection, understanding, and accountability, which can be instrumental in the recovery process.

Family therapy

Beyond personal and peer support, family therapy plays a crucial role in the healing journey. By involving loved ones in the treatment process, this approach helps to address family dynamics, improve communication, and create a supportive home environment for the individual in recovery.

Other outpatient treatment approaches include:

 

Lectures and process groups about the stages of change, family systems, and boundary education

Discussions of 12-step concepts for addiction treatment and codependency recovery

Education about coping methods for depression , anxiety , trauma , and PTSD 

Identifying triggers and managing impulsivity, along with exploring the costs and consequences of self-defeating behaviors

 

Engaging in IOP/PHP gives participants better long-term outcomes by preparing them to manage their own mental health and substance use disorders. In addition to teaching clients life skills like budgeting and community resource identification, they also learn coping mechanisms such as yoga, meditation, and recreation therapy.

 Call Now 

 

Our Tucson, Arizona, Outpatient Services 

A Virtual Intensive Outpatient program can be a beneficial option for many reasons. These programs are flexible and accessible, offering care from virtually anywhere. Our Cottonwood Virtual IOP is designed for those with mental health, substance use, or co-occurring disorders.

Here are some highlights of our Virtual IOP:

 

Offered three nights a week: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday from 5:30 – 8:30 pm.

Program participation usually lasts 8 to 12 weeks, depending on insurance coverage.

Features a therapeutic processing group, psychoeducation, and skill building.

Utilizes evidence-based modalities including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior

Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), and Moral Reconation Therapy (MRT).

The therapist will schedule a psychosocial assessment following a patient’s first group attendance.

 

Qualifications for admission to the Cottonwood Tucson Virtual IOP program include:

 

Being at least 18 years old.

A diagnosis of a mental health disorder or substance use disorder.

Being free from Suicidal or Homicidal Ideation

Having symptoms that interfere with social or vocational/educational functioning.

Being ready to step down from a residential or PHP program or step up from weekly individual or group outpatient treatment.

Having a computer with a camera and a microphone.

 

While a Virtual IOP is a great option, it may not be for everyone. If a patient begins to show mental instability during treatment, they will be assessed and referred for a higher level of care.

Start Your Outpatient Rehabilitation Journey Today 

Take the next step toward lasting addiction recovery today. Whether you need structured outpatient care or the flexibility of our Virtual IOP, we are here to support your journey. Contact us now to learn more about our programs and find the right path for your recovery.

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today. 

 Let Us Help You Get Started. 

 (888) 727-0441 Verify Your Insurance

class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p align="left">At Cottonwood Tucson, we rely on the latest research on the neurobiology of human development and the neuroscience of addiction and mental health treatment to design cutting-edge, patient-responsive treatment programs in Tucson, Arizona.</p>
<p>Finding the proper treatment for addiction and mental health in Arizona is essential for lasting recovery. At Cottonwood Tucson, we offer comprehensive, research-based programs designed to address the complex relationship between mental health and substance use disorders.</p>
<p><a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/staff/">Our clinical team</a> is multidisciplinary, highly trained, and experienced. For over 35 years, we’ve developed our holistic approach focused on treating the whole person—mind, body, and spirit—through personalized care plans that support long-term healing and wellness. Our understanding of the developing brain, and how addiction and mental health issues can affect the brain’s ability to make a content and confident mood, adds to our ability to treat these issues in adults successfully. This is called <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/co-occurring-disorders/">co-occurring disorders</a>: when a <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/">mental health issue</a> affects a <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/addiction-treatment/">substance use disorder</a>, and vice versa.</p>

class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-divider gdlr-core-skin-divider" ></span></h2></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr ;"  ><p>With over 35 years of experience, Cottonwood Tucson is proud to be a leading addiction treatment center in Tucson, Arizona. Our multidisciplinary team, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/holistic-treatment/">holistic approach</a>, and evidence-based practices offer a strong foundation for healing. From inpatient detox to virtual IOP, we meet patients wherever they are in their recovery journey.</p>
<h3>Start Your Path to Healing Today</h3>
<p>Recovery begins with the proper support, and we are here to help at Cottonwood Tucson. Our evidence-based mental health and substance abuse treatment programs in Arizona provide the care and guidance needed to overcome addiction and mental health challenges.</p>
<p>Our team is ready to help, whether you need residential treatment, outpatient services, or a personalized care plan for our mental health, alcohol, and drug abuse treatment program. <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/contact/">Contact us today</a> to take the first step toward lasting wellness.</p>

Our Addiction & Mental Health Treatment Programs in Tucson, AZ 

 
 

At Cottonwood Tucson, we rely on the latest research on the neurobiology of human development and the neuroscience of addiction and mental health treatment to design cutting-edge, patient-responsive treatment programs in Tucson, Arizona.

Finding the proper treatment for addiction and mental health in Arizona is essential for lasting recovery. At Cottonwood Tucson, we offer comprehensive, research-based programs designed to address the complex relationship between mental health and substance use disorders.

 Our clinical team is multidisciplinary, highly trained, and experienced. For over 35 years, we’ve developed our holistic approach focused on treating the whole person—mind, body, and spirit—through personalized care plans that support long-term healing and wellness. Our understanding of the developing brain, and how addiction and mental health issues can affect the brain’s ability to make a content and confident mood, adds to our ability to treat these issues in adults successfully. This is called co-occurring disorders : when a mental health issue affects a substance use disorder , and vice versa.

 Get Started 

What to Expect from Our Arizona Addiction Treatment & Mental Health Programs 

All patients who come to Cottonwood’s holistic treatment center in Tucson, Arizona, receive a thorough medical, psychiatric, and psychosocial assessment in their first few days of treatment. We can also provide cognitive, educational, and personality testing, and Eastern-based medical practices like acupuncture for patients who would benefit from them. Our comprehensive assessment program helps the clinical team create a personalized treatment plan optimally responsive to each patient’s unique needs.

 Call Now 

 Learn More 

Comprehensive Addiction & Mental Health Treatment Programs 

Cottonwood Assessment Program (CAP)

The Cottonwood Assessment Program (CAP) is a five-day intensive inpatient evaluation tailored for individuals with complex mental health or substance use concerns. It is particularly suited for those seeking diagnostic clarity, a second opinion, or support in overcoming denial related to behavioral or addiction issues.

 Assessment Program 

Residential Treatment Program

Our Residential Treatment Program offers 24/7 care in a structured, supportive environment to promote healing and long-term recovery. Clients benefit from personalized treatment plans, daily therapy sessions, and holistic wellness activities, all within a safe and comfortable setting that fosters growth away from the stressors of everyday life.

 Residential Treatment 

Medical Detox Services

Our Medical Detox program in Arizona provides safe, supervised withdrawal management for individuals beginning their recovery journey. Led by experienced medical professionals, our detox services are tailored to each client’s needs. 

 Medical Detox 

Outpatient Treatment Services (PHP & IOP)

Our Partial Hospitalization (PHP) and Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP) in Tucson, Arizona, offer flexible, structured outpatient treatment for individuals needing continued support but who do not require 24-hour care. With a combination of individual therapy, group counseling, and evidence-based treatment, clients can maintain their daily responsibilities while building a solid foundation for recovery.

 Outpatient Treatment 

Virtual IOP (Online Treatment)

Our Virtual Intensive Outpatient Program delivers the same high-quality care as in-person IOP through a secure, online platform. Designed for those who need flexible access to treatment in Arizona and nationwide, our Virtual IOP includes live group therapy, individual counseling, and ongoing support from licensed clinicians – all from the comfort of home.

 Virtual IOP 

Family Program for Addiction and Mental Health Recovery

Our Family Program is designed to educate, support, and empower loved ones throughout recovery. Through therapy, workshops, and guided communication, families learn how to heal together, rebuild trust, and create a healthier home environment that supports long-term recovery.

 Family Program 

Tactical Recovery – Veterans Treatment Program

 Tactical Recovery is our specialized treatment track for veterans, offering trauma-informed care in a community that understands military service. This program addresses the unique challenges veterans face, including PTSD, substance use, and reintegration, with personalized support designed to foster healing and resilience.

 Tactical Recovery 

Relapse Prevention for Long-Term Recovery

 Relapse prevention is a critical component of long-term recovery, which helps individuals identify triggers, manage cravings, and develop healthy coping strategies. Through therapy, education, and ongoing support, clients learn to recognize signs and build a personalized plan to maintain their progress. This proactive approach empowers individuals to stay grounded in recovery and navigate life’s challenges confidently.

 Relapse Prevention 

 

Addiction & Mental Health Conditions We Treat 

 

 Alcohol Addiction Treatment 

 Drug Addiction Treatment 

 Co-Occurring Disorders Treatment 

 Mental Health Treatment 

 Depression Treatment 

 Bipolar Disorder Treatment 

 Anxiety Disorder Treatment 

 Trauma & PTSD Treatment 

 Complicated Grief Treatment 

 

 Conditions We Treat 

Why Choose Cottonwood Tucson for Recovery in Arizona? 

With over 35 years of experience, Cottonwood Tucson is proud to be a leading addiction treatment center in Tucson, Arizona. Our multidisciplinary team, holistic approach , and evidence-based practices offer a strong foundation for healing. From inpatient detox to virtual IOP, we meet patients wherever they are in their recovery journey.

Start Your Path to Healing Today

Recovery begins with the proper support, and we are here to help at Cottonwood Tucson. Our evidence-based mental health and substance abuse treatment programs in Arizona provide the care and guidance needed to overcome addiction and mental health challenges.

Our team is ready to help, whether you need residential treatment, outpatient services, or a personalized care plan for our mental health, alcohol, and drug abuse treatment program. Contact us today to take the first step toward lasting wellness.

 

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today. 

 Let Us Help You Get Started. 

 (888) 727-0441 Verify Your Insurance

class="gdlr-core-pbf-column-content clearfix gdlr-core-js "   ><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr gdlr-core-item-pdb gdlr-core-left-align" style="padding-bottom: 0px ;"  ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item-content" style="text-transform: none ;color: #373737 ;"  ><p align="left">At Cottonwood Tucson, we rely on the latest research on the neurobiology of human development and the neuroscience of addiction and mental health treatment to design cutting-edge, patient-responsive treatment programs in Tucson, Arizona.</p>
<p>Finding the proper treatment for addiction and mental health in Arizona is essential for lasting recovery. At Cottonwood Tucson, we offer comprehensive, research-based programs designed to address the complex relationship between mental health and substance use disorders.</p>
<p><a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/staff/">Our clinical team</a> is multidisciplinary, highly trained, and experienced. For over 35 years, we’ve developed our holistic approach focused on treating the whole person—mind, body, and spirit—through personalized care plans that support long-term healing and wellness. Our understanding of the developing brain, and how addiction and mental health issues can affect the brain’s ability to make a content and confident mood, adds to our ability to treat these issues in adults successfully. This is called <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/co-occurring-disorders/">co-occurring disorders</a>: when a <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/mental-health-treatment/">mental health issue</a> affects a <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/addiction-treatment/">substance use disorder</a>, and vice versa.</p>

class="gdlr-core-title-item-title-divider gdlr-core-skin-divider" ></span></h2></div></div></div><div class="gdlr-core-pbf-element" ><div class="gdlr-core-text-box-item gdlr-core-item-pdlr ;"  ><p>With over 35 years of experience, Cottonwood Tucson is proud to be a leading addiction treatment center in Tucson, Arizona. Our multidisciplinary team, <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/holistic-treatment/">holistic approach</a>, and evidence-based practices offer a strong foundation for healing. From inpatient detox to virtual IOP, we meet patients wherever they are in their recovery journey.</p>
<h3>Start Your Path to Healing Today</h3>
<p>Recovery begins with the proper support, and we are here to help at Cottonwood Tucson. Our evidence-based mental health and substance abuse treatment programs in Arizona provide the care and guidance needed to overcome addiction and mental health challenges.</p>
<p>Our team is ready to help, whether you need residential treatment, outpatient services, or a personalized care plan for our mental health, alcohol, and drug abuse treatment program. <a href="https://cottonwooddetucson.com/contact/">Contact us today</a> to take the first step toward lasting wellness.</p>

Our Addiction & Mental Health Treatment Programs in Tucson, AZ 

 
 

At Cottonwood Tucson, we rely on the latest research on the neurobiology of human development and the neuroscience of addiction and mental health treatment to design cutting-edge, patient-responsive treatment programs in Tucson, Arizona.

Finding the proper treatment for addiction and mental health in Arizona is essential for lasting recovery. At Cottonwood Tucson, we offer comprehensive, research-based programs designed to address the complex relationship between mental health and substance use disorders.

 Our clinical team is multidisciplinary, highly trained, and experienced. For over 35 years, we’ve developed our holistic approach focused on treating the whole person—mind, body, and spirit—through personalized care plans that support long-term healing and wellness. Our understanding of the developing brain, and how addiction and mental health issues can affect the brain’s ability to make a content and confident mood, adds to our ability to treat these issues in adults successfully. This is called co-occurring disorders : when a mental health issue affects a substance use disorder , and vice versa.

 Get Started 

What to Expect from Our Arizona Addiction Treatment & Mental Health Programs 

All patients who come to Cottonwood’s holistic treatment center in Tucson, Arizona, receive a thorough medical, psychiatric, and psychosocial assessment in their first few days of treatment. We can also provide cognitive, educational, and personality testing, and Eastern-based medical practices like acupuncture for patients who would benefit from them. Our comprehensive assessment program helps the clinical team create a personalized treatment plan optimally responsive to each patient’s unique needs.

 Call Now 

 Learn More 

Comprehensive Addiction & Mental Health Treatment Programs 

Cottonwood Assessment Program (CAP)

The Cottonwood Assessment Program (CAP) is a five-day intensive inpatient evaluation tailored for individuals with complex mental health or substance use concerns. It is particularly suited for those seeking diagnostic clarity, a second opinion, or support in overcoming denial related to behavioral or addiction issues.

 Assessment Program 

Residential Treatment Program

Our Residential Treatment Program offers 24/7 care in a structured, supportive environment to promote healing and long-term recovery. Clients benefit from personalized treatment plans, daily therapy sessions, and holistic wellness activities, all within a safe and comfortable setting that fosters growth away from the stressors of everyday life.

 Residential Treatment 

Medical Detox Services

Our Medical Detox program in Arizona provides safe, supervised withdrawal management for individuals beginning their recovery journey. Led by experienced medical professionals, our detox services are tailored to each client’s needs. 

 Medical Detox 

Outpatient Treatment Services (PHP & IOP)

Our Partial Hospitalization (PHP) and Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP) in Tucson, Arizona, offer flexible, structured outpatient treatment for individuals needing continued support but who do not require 24-hour care. With a combination of individual therapy, group counseling, and evidence-based treatment, clients can maintain their daily responsibilities while building a solid foundation for recovery.

 Outpatient Treatment 

Virtual IOP (Online Treatment)

Our Virtual Intensive Outpatient Program delivers the same high-quality care as in-person IOP through a secure, online platform. Designed for those who need flexible access to treatment in Arizona and nationwide, our Virtual IOP includes live group therapy, individual counseling, and ongoing support from licensed clinicians – all from the comfort of home.

 Virtual IOP 

Family Program for Addiction and Mental Health Recovery

Our Family Program is designed to educate, support, and empower loved ones throughout recovery. Through therapy, workshops, and guided communication, families learn how to heal together, rebuild trust, and create a healthier home environment that supports long-term recovery.

 Family Program 

Tactical Recovery – Veterans Treatment Program

 Tactical Recovery is our specialized treatment track for veterans, offering trauma-informed care in a community that understands military service. This program addresses the unique challenges veterans face, including PTSD, substance use, and reintegration, with personalized support designed to foster healing and resilience.

 Tactical Recovery 

Relapse Prevention for Long-Term Recovery

 Relapse prevention is a critical component of long-term recovery, which helps individuals identify triggers, manage cravings, and develop healthy coping strategies. Through therapy, education, and ongoing support, clients learn to recognize signs and build a personalized plan to maintain their progress. This proactive approach empowers individuals to stay grounded in recovery and navigate life’s challenges confidently.

 Relapse Prevention 

 

Addiction & Mental Health Conditions We Treat 

 

 Alcohol Addiction Treatment 

 Drug Addiction Treatment 

 Co-Occurring Disorders Treatment 

 Mental Health Treatment 

 Depression Treatment 

 Bipolar Disorder Treatment 

 Anxiety Disorder Treatment 

 Trauma & PTSD Treatment 

 Complicated Grief Treatment 

 

 Conditions We Treat 

Why Choose Cottonwood Tucson for Recovery in Arizona? 

With over 35 years of experience, Cottonwood Tucson is proud to be a leading addiction treatment center in Tucson, Arizona. Our multidisciplinary team, holistic approach , and evidence-based practices offer a strong foundation for healing. From inpatient detox to virtual IOP, we meet patients wherever they are in their recovery journey.

Start Your Path to Healing Today

Recovery begins with the proper support, and we are here to help at Cottonwood Tucson. Our evidence-based mental health and substance abuse treatment programs in Arizona provide the care and guidance needed to overcome addiction and mental health challenges.

Our team is ready to help, whether you need residential treatment, outpatient services, or a personalized care plan for our mental health, alcohol, and drug abuse treatment program. Contact us today to take the first step toward lasting wellness.

 

Begin Your New Life in Recovery Today. 

 Let Us Help You Get Started. 

 (888) 727-0441 Verify Your Insurance
